Congratulations Jorge! You're On Your Way to Being Enrolled.

Thank you for starting your education journey with APU! Complete the remaining steps below to start the enrollment process. Our admissions team is here to help if you have any questions as you continue to gather items needed in order to enroll before the next start date.

Upload Photo ID

Please upload a clear, legible copy of a government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, or state ID).

Click to upload or drag and drop

PNG, JPG, or PDF (max 10MB)

Your ID will be securely stored and only used for verification purposes.

Opt Out of Transfer Credit Evaluation

You previously indicated that you have college credits from another institution that may be transferable to APUS.

Important: If you opt out of the transfer credit evaluation process, any previously earned college credits will not be evaluated or applied toward your degree requirements at APUS.

By opting out, you understand that:

  • You will not receive credit for courses completed at other institutions
  • You may need to retake courses you have already completed
  • Your time to degree completion may be longer
  • You can request a transfer credit evaluation later, but processing times may vary

Are you sure you want to opt out of the transfer credit evaluation?

Change Your Program

Note: Changing your program may affect your enrollment timeline and transfer credit evaluation. Please review your selection carefully.

Important: Your program change request will be reviewed by an academic advisor. You will receive a confirmation email within 2-3 business days.

Academic Plan: Master of Arts in History

Core Courses (24 Credit Hours)

36
Total Credit Hours
8
Core Courses
24 credits
3
Elective Courses
9 credits
1
Capstone Project
3 credits
18-24
Months to Complete
All courses are 8 weeks in length

Core Courses (24 Credit Hours)

HIST 501 - Historical Research Methods

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 502 - Historiography

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 510 - American History Since 1865

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 520 - European History: 1500-Present

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 530 - World History: Modern Era

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 540 - Military History

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 550 - Public History

3 Credit Hours

Core

HIST 560 - Digital History and Technology

3 Credit Hours

Core

Elective Courses (Choose 9 Credit Hours)

HIST 610 - American Revolution and Early Republic

3 Credit Hours

Elective

HIST 620 - Civil War and Reconstruction

3 Credit Hours

Elective

HIST 630 - Ancient Civilizations

3 Credit Hours

Elective

HIST 640 - Medieval Europe

3 Credit Hours

Elective

HIST 650 - Asian History and Culture

3 Credit Hours

Elective

HIST 660 - Latin American History

3 Credit Hours

Elective

Capstone Project (3 Credit Hours)

HIST 690 - Master's Capstone Project

3 Credit Hours

Capstone
Exit Financing Wizard

Learn More About Your Financing Options

Explore the various ways you can finance your education at APU. Each option has different requirements and benefits. Select a financing option below to learn more about eligibility, application process, and how to get started.

Please Note: Your selection can be changed later. This answer is optional and purely for informational purposes.

Selected Financing Option:


Change Selection

Military TA

Military Tuition Assistance (TA) is a benefit paid to eligible members of the Army, Navy, Marines, Air Force, and Coast Guard. TA is available for active duty, National Guard, and Reserve Component service members.

Eligibility: Active duty service members and eligible Reserve/Guard members.

Coverage: Covers up to 100% of tuition costs, subject to annual caps per fiscal year.

Employer Benefits

Many employers offer tuition assistance or reimbursement programs to help employees pursue higher education. These programs can significantly reduce your out-of-pocket costs.

Eligibility: Check with your employer's HR department for specific eligibility requirements.

Coverage: Varies by employer; some cover a percentage of tuition, while others offer a fixed annual amount.

Federal Student Aid

Federal Student Aid includes grants, loans, and work-study programs provided by the U.S. Department of Education. Complete the FAFSA to determine your eligibility.

Eligibility: Based on financial need, enrollment status, and other factors determined by the FAFSA.

Coverage: Includes Pell Grants, Direct Loans, and Federal Work-Study opportunities.

GI Bill® Benefits

The GI Bill® provides educational benefits to veterans, service members, and their families. Benefits include tuition coverage, monthly housing allowances, and stipends for books and supplies.

Eligibility: Veterans with qualifying active duty service and eligible dependents.

Coverage: Covers tuition and fees, plus provides monthly housing allowance and book stipends.

Self-Payment

Pay for your education directly out-of-pocket. APU offers flexible payment plans to help make tuition more manageable.

Eligibility: Available to all students.

Coverage: You pay the full tuition amount. Payment plans are available to spread costs over time.

VR&E (Veteran Readiness and Employment)

VR&E provides services to veterans with service-connected disabilities to help them prepare for, find, and maintain suitable employment or achieve independence in daily living.

Eligibility: Veterans with service-connected disabilities rated at least 10% by the VA.

Coverage: Covers tuition, fees, books, supplies, and may include subsistence allowance.

MyCAA

The Military Spouse Career Advancement Accounts (MyCAA) program provides up to $4,000 of tuition assistance to eligible military spouses pursuing licenses, certifications, or associate degrees.

Eligibility: Spouses of active duty service members in pay grades E-1 to E-5, W-1 to W-2, and O-1 to O-2.

Coverage: Up to $4,000 in financial assistance (fiscal year 2023 cap: $2,000 per year).

Scholarship

APU offers a variety of scholarships to help reduce the cost of education. Scholarships are awarded based on merit, financial need, or specific criteria.

Eligibility: Varies by scholarship; some are merit-based, while others are need-based or targeted to specific groups.

Coverage: Award amounts vary by scholarship program.

Private Loan

Private student loans are offered by banks, credit unions, and other financial institutions. These loans can help cover the gap between your education costs and other financial aid.

Eligibility: Based on creditworthiness; may require a co-signer if you have limited credit history.

Coverage: Loan amounts vary by lender and are subject to credit approval.

ArmyIgnitED Guide

ArmyIgnitED is the Army's education portal that allows soldiers to manage their educational benefits, including TA requests, credentialing programs, and tuition assistance.

Eligibility: Active duty Army soldiers and eligible Army Reserve/Guard members.

Coverage: Access to Army TA, which covers up to 100% of tuition costs subject to annual caps.

Not Sure Yet?

That's okay! We're here to help you explore all your options. Our financial aid advisors can guide you through the various financing options available and help you determine which is best for your situation.

Next Steps: Schedule a consultation with a financial aid advisor to discuss your options.

Contact: Reach out to our financial aid office at financialaid@apus.edu or call 1-877-468-6268.

Do Not Sell My Personal Information (CA residents only)

American Public University System (APUS) is accredited by the Higher Learning Commission (HLC) and is a wholly owned subsidiary of American Public Education, Inc. (APEI). Pursuant to a contractual arrangement, APEI provides certain services for the benefit of APUS, subject to APUS oversight and ultimate authority. Pursuant to a license, APEI may use APUS's trademarks and other intellectual property in its performance of those services. American Public University System, American Public University, and American Military University are not affiliated with American University or the U.S. Military.
